title: plantago lanceolata subsp. lacustris
intro: Plantago lanceolata subsp. lacustris, also known as the lake plantain, is a subspecies of the common plantain (Plantago lanceolata) that is typically found in wetlands and near bodies of water. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health conditions, including wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of lake plantain include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of lake plantain often include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
